2604.17340.title: Neuro-Symbolic Resolution of Recommendation Conflicts in Multimorbidity Clinical Guidelines
2604.17340.paper_summary: Clinical guidelines, typically developed by independent specialty societies, inherently exhibit substantial fragmentation, redundancy, and logical contradiction. These inconsistencies, particularly when applied to patients with multimorbidity, not only cause cognitive dissonance for clinicians but also introduce catastrophic noise into AI systems, rendering the standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system fragile and prone to hallucination. To address this fundamental reliability crisis, we introduce a Neuro-Symbolic framework that automates the detection of recommendation redundancies and conflicts. Our pipeline employs a multi-agent system to translate unstructured clinical natural language into rigorous symbolic logic language, which is then verified by a Satisfiability (SAT) solver. By formulating a hierarchical taxonomy of logical rule interactions, we identify a critical category termed Local Conflict - a decision conflict arising from the intersection of comorbidities. Evaluating our system on a curated benchmark of 12 authoritative SGLT2 inhibitor guidelines, we reveal that 90.6% of conflicts are Local, a structural complexity that single-disease guidelines fail to address. While state-of-the-art LLMs fail in detecting these conflicts, our neuro-symbolic approach achieves an F1 score of 0.861. This work demonstrates that logical verification must precede retrieval, establishing a new technical standard for automated knowledge coordination in medical AI.
2604.17340.paper_summary_zh: 臨床指導方針通常由獨立的專業協會制定，固有地顯示出相當大的碎片化、冗餘和邏輯矛盾。這些不一致性，特別是在應用於多重疾病患者時，不僅會給臨床醫生帶來認知失調，還會為人工智慧系統引入災難性的噪音，使標準的檢索增強生成（RAG）系統變得脆弱並容易產生幻覺。為了解決這一基本的可靠性危機，我們提出了一個神經符號框架，該框架自動檢測建議的冗餘和衝突。我們的管道使用多智能體系統將非結構化的臨床自然語言轉換為嚴謹的符號邏輯語言，然後由可滿足性（SAT）求解器進行驗證。通過制定邏輯規則互動的分層分類法，我們確定了一個關鍵類別，稱為局部衝突——這是一種由共病交集引起的決策衝突。在對12個權威的SGLT2抑制劑指導方針進行的精心策劃的基準評估中，我們揭示了90.6%的衝突是局部的，這是一種單一疾病指導方針未能解決的結構性複雜性。雖然最先進的LLM在檢測這些衝突方面失敗，我們的神經符號方法達到了0.861的F1分數。這項工作表明，邏輯驗證必須在檢索之前進行，為醫療人工智慧中的自動知識協調建立了一個新的技術標準。
2604.17340.author: Shiyao Xie et.al.
